description:
Goal: make compiler2 struct definitions and struct-field references follow the same reference/definition obligation model that works for module callables and type definitions. A reference to struct module A and fields on A must be recordable before A is processed; consumers that need field layout wait on a precise struct fact; diagnostics for missing fields are emitted when A settles, using requester spans from the referencing code.

Current code shape:
- src/compiler2/source_publish.rs parses ScopeForm::Struct but apply_scope_form currently treats ScopeForm::Struct(_) as no output. defstruct is embedded in module source, not published as its own compiler2 fact.
- src/compiler2/world.rs module_struct_fields(module) and struct_schemas() scan ModuleState source forms for ScopeForm::Struct. This makes ModuleDefined/Indexed act as an overbroad proxy for schema availability.
- src/compiler2/resolve.rs TypeExpr::StructRecord resolves %Struct{} by opportunistically reading module_struct_fields; when schema is absent it uses source field order, and unknown fields can be silently dropped once schema order exists.
- src/compiler2/source_publish.rs collect_type_refs walks field value types inside StructRecord but does not record the struct module reference or field obligations.
- src/compiler2/jobs/body.rs lower_struct_expr and lower_struct_pattern synchronously require module_struct_fields and emit fatal unsupported-schema diagnostics instead of waiting on a precise StructDefined fact; they also validate missing fields locally instead of through durable obligations.
- src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs struct_assertion_ty and struct construction ask World for module_struct_fields; protocol dispatch in jobs/semantic.rs calls World::module_impl_target_ty, which currently classifies targets by last-segment strings plus module_struct_fields.
- src/compiler2/jobs/backend.rs builds BackendProgram.struct_schemas from World::struct_schemas; ir_interp/native/codegen consume that schema registry at MakeStruct / StructField time, so backend schema materialization must be fed from the new struct-definition store.

Desired model:
- Add a first-class StructDef store keyed by ModuleId, with ordered fields and source/requester provenance as needed.
- Add FactKey::StructDefined(ModuleId), published when a module body containing defstruct settles. No ScopeForm::Struct no-op path.
- Add a durable StructFieldExpectation / StructReference obligation store. Each use records module, field, requester code/module/span, and usage kind such as literal, pattern, type expression, field access when statically known, or protocol impl target classification if needed.
- Referencing %A{first: ...} from module B must reference A cheaply and record A.first even if A has not been processed. If the current job needs layout/order, it waits on StructDefined(A) and schedules the module job that can publish it.
- When StructDefined(A) is published, validate all outstanding expectations for A against the schema and emit diagnostics at requester spans for missing fields. This mirrors module interface expectation validation.
- Replace module_struct_fields source scanning with accessors over the StructDef store, then remove the old scan path.
- Replace stringly protocol impl target classification with a typed target classification backed by facts: builtin value family vs Struct(ModuleId) vs non-struct nominal target if that remains meaningful. module_impl_target_ty should project from that typed kind to Ty.
- Type resolution for %Struct{} should wait/read StructDefined when precise schema/order is needed, and must not silently drop unknown fields.
- Backend struct_schemas should come from the StructDef store/facts, not ModuleState source scanning.

Tests required:
- Out-of-order module test: module B references %A{field: ...} before module A is defined/processed; the first pass records obligations and waits only on StructDefined(A), then succeeds after A publishes defstruct.
- Missing field test: B references %A{missing: ...}; no immediate unsupported-schema fatal if A is pending; after A settles, diagnostic points to B field usage.
- Type-expression test: @type t :: %A{field: integer} records A.field and waits/reads StructDefined(A); unknown fields are diagnosed rather than dropped.
- Pattern/literal lowering tests: struct literal and struct pattern consume ordered schema from StructDefined(A), not ModuleDefined(A) or module-source scans.
- Protocol dispatch test: defimpl P, for: A does not classify A by string; if A is a struct, impl-target type includes nominal identity plus tuple/map field evidence through the typed struct target path.
- Backend/interpreter smoke: BackendProgram.struct_schemas is populated from StructDef facts and MakeStruct/StructField continue to run.

Docs:
- Update .agent/docs/compiler2-migration.md or the relevant compiler2 agent doc with the invariant: struct names and fields are reference obligations; StructDefined is the minimal fact for schema/layout; ModuleDefined is not schema authority.

Success signal:
- No code path scans ModuleState source forms for struct schemas after publication.
- Jobs wait on StructDefined(A) when they need layout and only record field obligations when they do not.
- Missing struct fields are reported from obligation validation with the referencing span, independent of module processing order.
- module_impl_target_ty no longer decodes semantics from module-name strings.
title: Compiler2 structs: publish field-schema facts and validate field reference obligations
